title: Timeline Editor — WaveformPlayer, WaveformEditor, Cut Regions, Version Submit
problem: Band members need to trim dead air and setup noise from a recording — visually, in a browser, on a phone in a car or on a laptop at home — and preview the result before committing it as a new version. The clip detail page needs a pure playback experience that skips cuts during listen; the edit page needs a full-screen editor for creating cut regions.
analysis:
Two dedicated components with separate concerns:

**WaveformPlayer** (clip detail page) — pure playback. Receives activeCuts from parent (ClipDetailClient). Visualises cuts as blue non-draggable regions (pointerEvents: none on regions so they don't block click-to-seek). Skip-cuts during playback via timeupdate handler. H:MM:SS clock + virtual duration display. Stamp chips are tappable <button> elements — each seeks to that timestamp. Big 64px play button.

**WaveformEditor** (edit page, always edit mode) — cut creation only. Custom pointer overlay (absolute inset-0, touch-none) handles both tap-to-seek and drag-to-create-region via onPointerDown/Move/Up + setPointerCapture. Red draggable/resizable regions. Trim shortcut (pre-seeds start+end cuts). Clear All button. Split mode (mark a time position, confirm → creates Part B as new clip, navigates to session page). 'Start from' version pills pre-load a previous version's cuts. Submit → new version in Postgres, router.back(). Draft auto-saved to localStorage on every cut change; draft banner with Resume/Dismiss on reopen.

Audio for both is served from /api/audio/[clipId] — a Next.js route that proxies Drive with Range request support, forwarding the user's stored token server-side. WaveSurfer interact: true enables click-to-seek in both components.
successCriteria:
- Waveform renders within 3 seconds of opening on a typical 10-min clip
- Audio plays via Drive proxied URL — seeking supported via Range requests
- Click-to-seek works — clicking anywhere on the waveform moves the playhead; play starts from that position
- Stamp chips in WaveformPlayer are tappable <button> elements that seek to their timestamp
- Cut regions in WaveformPlayer are blue overlays with pointerEvents:none — they display cuts but never block click-to-seek
- Skip-cuts playback works in WaveformPlayer: entering a cut region during play immediately jumps to region end
- Drag on the WaveformEditor waveform creates a red cut region
- Tap (< 8px movement) in WaveformEditor seeks without creating a region
- Trim button pre-seeds start cut + end cut from clip boundaries
- Clear All button removes all cuts
- Split: marks a time → creates Part B clip + v2 on original → navigates to session page showing both
- 'Start from vN' pills load a previous version's cuts as starting point
- Submit button always active — description optional
- Draft auto-saved on every cut change; Resume/Dismiss banner shown on reopen after crash
- Tab bar hidden during editing (edit page is near full-screen)
- Virtual result duration updates in real time in WaveformEditor top bar
implementation:
1. components/clips/WaveformPlayer.tsx: pure player. Props: clipId, sourceDurationMs, activeCuts[], stamps[]. On init: WaveSurfer.create({ interact: true }). After addRegion(): region.element.style.pointerEvents = 'none' (display-only, does not block seek clicks). timeupdate handler skips cuts by calling ws.setTime(cut.endMs/1000). Stamp chips rendered as <button> elements that call ws.setTime(stamp.timestampMs/1000). 64px circular play/pause button.
2. components/clips/WaveformEditor.tsx: edit-only component. Props: clipId, sourceDurationMs, frozenVersionId, initialVersions, stamps, sessionHref. Custom pointer overlay: absolute inset-0 div with touch-action:none + onPointerDown/Move/Up + setPointerCapture(pointerId). Tap (delta < 8px) → ws.seekTo(ratio); drag → regionsRef.addRegion(). No enableDragSelection() call. Trim button: addRegion({start:0, end:firstGapStart}) + addRegion({start:lastGapEnd, end:duration}). Clear All: removes all regions. Split: prompts for split time, calls POST /api/clips/[clipId]/split, navigates to sessionHref. 'Start from' pills in bottom sheet load version.cutMarks into state. localStorage key: odio:draft:{clipId}. Draft banner: Resume loads cuts, Dismiss clears localStorage. router.back() after version submit.
3. app/api/audio/[clipId]/route.ts: proxies Drive audio with Range header forwarding. Fetches token from NextAuth accounts table for current user's bandId.
4. lib/utils.ts: formatPosition(ms) → H:MM:SS always. formatDuration(ms) → M:SS. calcResultDuration(sourceDurationMs, cuts) → virtual result duration.

decisions.decision: WaveformPlayer and WaveformEditor are separate components
decisions.rationale: A single component handling both playback and editing requires mode-switching logic, conditional overlays, and competing event handlers. Separate components have clear single responsibilities: Player = listen, Editor = cut. The edit page is always in edit mode; no toggle needed. The detail page never needs editing. Each component can be reasoned about and tested independently.
decisions.recommendation: WaveformPlayer lives on clip detail and share pages. WaveformEditor lives exclusively on the /edit page. ClipDetailClient orchestrates state shared between Player and other sections.

decisions.decision: Custom pointer overlay for drag-to-create in editor (no enableDragSelection)
decisions.rationale: WaveSurfer's enableDragSelection registers a permanent pointer overlay that consumes all pointer events — including click-to-seek. Once called, seeking by clicking is broken. A custom absolute-positioned overlay with onPointerDown/Move/Up + setPointerCapture handles both tap-to-seek (< 8px movement) and drag-to-create (>= 8px) without blocking WaveSurfer's own event handling on other elements.
decisions.recommendation: In WaveformEditor: absolute inset-0 div with touch-action:none as overlay. tap → seekTo(); drag → addRegion(). No enableDragSelection() call anywhere.

decisions.decision: pointerEvents:none on display-only regions in WaveformPlayer
decisions.rationale: WaveSurfer RegionsPlugin renders region overlays as absolutely positioned divs above the waveform canvas in z-index. Without pointerEvents:none, clicking on a cut region (the blue overlay) is swallowed by the div before reaching WaveSurfer's click-to-seek handler. The cursor moves but the playhead doesn't update.
decisions.recommendation: After addRegion() in WaveformPlayer, set region.element.style.pointerEvents = 'none'. This makes all regions display-only and lets all clicks pass through to WaveSurfer's seek handler.

decisions.decision: Stamp chips in WaveformPlayer are tappable buttons, not divs
decisions.rationale: Divs are not keyboard-accessible and do not receive touch events reliably on all browsers. Using <button> elements with explicit onClick={() => ws.setTime(stamp.timestampMs/1000)} makes stamp chips proper interactive controls with correct accessibility semantics and reliable touch behavior.
decisions.recommendation: Render stamp chips as <button> with aria-label='Jump to M:SS'. onClick calls ws.setTime(stamp.timestampMs/1000). Style with active:scale-95 for tap feedback.

decisions.decision: Split navigates to session page, not back to clip detail
decisions.rationale: After a split, two clips exist. router.back() would go back to the original clip's detail page — the user would not see Part B at all without manually navigating. router.push(sessionHref) lands on the session clip list where both Part A and Part B are visible and accessible.
decisions.recommendation: WaveformEditor accepts sessionHref prop. After successful split API call: router.push(sessionHref).

decisions.decision: Virtual cut preview via seek — no re-encode
decisions.rationale: Re-encoding for preview requires server round-trip or WASM FFmpeg. WaveSurfer can seek to any position — skip cut regions during playback by intercepting timeupdate events. Zero server cost, instant response.
decisions.recommendation: Intercept WaveSurfer 'timeupdate': if currentTime falls within a cut region, seek to region.endMs immediately.

decisions.decision: Drive audio proxied through Next.js API route
decisions.rationale: Drive API download URLs require an Authorization header with the user's access token. Browsers cannot add auth headers to an <audio> src or WaveSurfer source URL. Proxying through a Next.js API route (which has the token server-side) solves this without exposing the token to the client.
decisions.recommendation: GET /api/audio/{clipId} → server fetches from Drive with token → streams response to client. Supports Range requests for seeking.

decisions.rationale: Full offline sync adds complexity. The real risk is a browser crash mid-edit. localStorage persists cut marks across crashes. Key: odio:draft:{clipId}. Cleared on successful submit.
decisions.recommendation: WaveformEditor writes cutMarks to localStorage on every change. Draft banner shown on reopen. Resume loads draft; Dismiss clears it.

knownLimitations: Editor overlay blocks region resize handles on existing cuts — to adjust a cut, remove it and redraw
Audio proxy route adds latency vs direct URL — mitigated by the server having fast egress to Google's CDN
If the clip's Drive file is unavailable (recorder revoked share, Drive outage), editor shows an error
userGuide: From a clip's detail page, the waveform player shows cuts as blue overlays. Tap any stamp chip to jump to that moment. Click-to-seek works anywhere on the waveform — even on cut overlays. Tap 'Edit cuts' to open the full-screen editor. In the editor: drag on the waveform to create a red cut region; tap Trim to pre-seed start/end cuts; tap Clear all to reset; tap Split to divide the song into two clips at a chosen time position. Tap 'Start from vN' to load a previous version's cuts as a starting point. Tap 'Submit edit' to save as a new version — description is optional. Draft cuts survive browser crashes and are recovered on reopen.

statusHistory.reason: Major architectural redesign: WaveformPlayer (pure playback) split from WaveformEditor (pure edit). Custom pointer overlay replaces enableDragSelection. pointerEvents:none on display-only regions fixes click-to-seek. Stamp chips changed to tappable buttons. Split navigates to session page. Trim/Clear All/Split buttons added.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Separated WaveformPlayer (pure playback, pointerEvents:none on regions, tappable stamp chips) from WaveformEditor (custom pointer overlay, no enableDragSelection, Trim/Clear/Split tools, localStorage draft banner, sessionHref for split navigation).
